prompt: Implement issue #624: complete Jitsi CRDs, core controllers, webhook sync, agent bridge integration, and event bus coverage. The current staging branch may already contain partial Jitsi resource-model, web route, CLI/MCP, and test work; audit and preserve complete surfaces before filling gaps.

observedPartialSurfaces: packages/krate/core/src/resource-model.js contains JitsiMeetProvider, JitsiMeetingTemplate, JitsiMeeting, and JitsiRecording entries on current staging.
packages/krate/web/app/api/orgs/[org]/jitsi/** routes exist on current staging, including webhooks/ingest.
packages/krate/web/app/lib/jitsi-service.js and Jitsi web components exist on current staging.
packages/krate/cli/src/mcp-server.js and packages/krate/cli/tests/jitsi-mcp-tools.test.js include Jitsi meeting MCP surfaces on current staging.
observedLikelyGaps: The current Jitsi webhook route appears lightweight and must be audited for timing-safe HMAC validation, persistent deduplication, replay handling, participant events, room destroyed events, and recording stopped events.
Chart CRD manifests and package exports must be verified rather than assumed complete.

expectedScope: Verify and complete JitsiMeetProvider and JitsiMeetingTemplate as etcd/config resource kinds.
Verify and complete JitsiMeeting and JitsiRecording as postgres/aggregated resource kinds.
Verify and complete chart CRDs, package exports, schemas, and tests for the new Jitsi resource and controller surfaces.
Implement jitsi-meeting-controller.js with validate, createRoom, endRoom, generateParticipantJwt, reconcile, listActiveMeetings, getMeetingStats, startRecording, and stopRecording.
Implement jitsi-sync-controller.js following the external sync-controller pattern for Jitsi room, participant, recording, idempotency, and watermark sync.
Implement jitsi-agent-bridge.js with meeting capability checks, meeting context preparation, sidecar spec creation, and agent join/leave handling.
Harden /api/orgs/[org]/jitsi/webhooks/ingest with timing-safe signature validation, delivery deduplication/replay protection, event normalization, and controller delegation.
Emit or persist meeting-created, participant-joined, participant-left, recording-started, and agent-joined-meeting through the existing event bus shape.
Keep Jitsi Helm deployment work in #623, web-console management UX in #625, and deeper agent meeting runtime work in #627.
qualityGates: Phase 0 reuse audit rendered before implementation planning.
Acceptance and unit tests authored or strengthened before production code.
All four Jitsi resource kinds verified for storage, context, plural, requiredSpec, schema, and API behavior.
Jitsi meeting, sync, and agent bridge controllers covered by focused unit tests with mocked providers and resource gateways.
Webhook security negative tests cover missing, malformed, invalid, replayed, duplicate, and invalid JSON cases.
Room, participant, and recording webhook events update resources idempotently.
Event bus coverage verifies meeting-created, participant-joined, participant-left, recording-started, and agent-joined-meeting.
Non-meeting agent dispatch behavior has a regression guard.
Focused Krate tests plus npm run test:sdk, npm run build:sdk, and npm run verify:metadata are run or exact blockers are documented.
Final review compares the issue and Jitsi specs directly against the git diff and verification output before delivery.
outOfScope: Do not implement or alter Jitsi Helm deployment work owned by #623 except for CRD manifests required by #624.
Do not redesign Jitsi web-console management UX owned by #625.
Do not implement deep agent meeting runtime, sidecar protocol, audio processing, or MCP runtime work owned by #627.
Do not require a live Jitsi server for unit or contract tests; use injectable mocked providers first.
